Bradenton Home Loans

FHA loans in Bradenton, Florida

Bradenton offers a more affordable alternative to neighboring Sarasota, with established West Bradenton neighborhoods and the booming, master-planned Lakewood Ranch area nearby. That mix of value and new construction makes it a strong FHA market: the statewide loan limit covers most homes, and 3.5% down works well for first-time buyers and families moving to the Gulf Coast.

Manatee County FHA limit (1-unit)$541,287

2026 single-family

Minimum down3.5%

With a 580+ score

Min credit score580

500–579 with 10% down

Property types1–4 units

Owner-occupied

What the FHA limit means in Bradenton

With the 2026 FHA limit of $541,287 and FHA's 3.5% minimum down, you can finance a Bradenton home priced up to roughly $560,000 (your loan covers the price minus your down payment). In Bradenton's relatively affordable market, that covers most homes, including many new builds in the Lakewood Ranch area. A few Florida counties are set higher, so confirm Manatee County's exact figure with HUD.

Value and new construction in the Bradenton area

Bradenton lets Gulf Coast buyers get more for their money than in Sarasota, and the Lakewood Ranch area's new construction is tailor-made for FHA — newer homes pass the appraisal easily and sit within the loan limit, ideal for first-time buyers. Pair FHA with Florida assistance and gift funds, and we'll get you pre-approved before you shop.

Down payment help for Bradenton buyers

You don't have to cover the down payment alone. FHA allows gift funds, sellers can contribute up to 6% toward closing costs, and Bradenton buyers can often pair FHA with assistance from the Florida Housing Finance Corporation and Manatee County's local SHIP-funded programs. Funding and eligibility change frequently, so we'll help you confirm what's currently available — see our FHA down payment assistance guide.

Neighborhoods we serve in Bradenton

We help buyers throughout Bradenton and Manatee County, including West Bradenton, the Lakewood Ranch area, and Palma Sola. Wherever you're looking, we'll explain your FHA loan limit, rate, and monthly payment — and get you pre-approved fast.

Questions & Answers

Frequently asked questions

What is the FHA loan limit in Bradenton (Manatee County) for 2026?+
Manatee County is at the 2026 Florida FHA floor of $541,287, which covers most Bradenton homes. Confirm the exact figure with HUD's official lookup.
Is Bradenton more affordable than Sarasota for FHA buyers?+
Generally yes — Bradenton tends to offer more value, which means FHA's loan limit stretches further across the local market.
Does FHA work for Lakewood Ranch-area new construction?+
Yes — newer homes typically pass the FHA appraisal easily and sit within the loan limit.
How much down payment do I need in Bradenton?+
As little as 3.5% with a 580+ score, reduced with gift funds and Florida assistance.
Do you serve all of Manatee County?+
Yes — Bradenton, the Lakewood Ranch area, Palmetto, and surrounding communities.
